description: BIS Standard for Toilet Soap — IS 2888:2004 including EU Cosmetics Regulation group limits for fragrance and colorants.

rules.name: TFM (Total Fatty Matter)
rules.description: Total Fatty Matter must be ≥76% for Grade 1 toilet soap (BIS IS 2888:2004, Cl. 3.1).

rules.name: Moisture Content
rules.description: Moisture must not exceed 15% to prevent microbial growth and maintain bar hardness.

rules.name: Free Alkali (NaOH equivalent)
rules.description: Free alkali (as NaOH) must not exceed 0.05% — excess alkali causes skin irritation (BIS IS 2888:2004, Cl. 3.3).

rules.name: Mercury Ban
rules.description: Mercury and mercury compounds are strictly banned in cosmetics and soap under BIS, EU, and FSSAI regulations.

rules.name: Lead Limit
rules.description: Lead (Pb) must not exceed 20 ppm in cosmetics — EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex II, permissible trace level.

rules.name: Arsenic Limit
rules.description: Arsenic (As) trace limit in soap — maximum 2 ppm (2 mg/kg) as per BIS heavy metals guidance.

rules.name: Triclosan Limit
rules.description: Triclosan (antibacterial) must not exceed 0.3% in cosmetics — EU Cosmetics Regulation 1223/2009.

rules.name: Formaldehyde Ban
rules.description: Free Formaldehyde in wash-off cosmetics must not exceed 0.1% (1000 ppm) — EU Cosmetics Reg Annex V.

rules.name: Rosin Limit
rules.description: Rosin (colophony) is a sensitizer — should not exceed 1% in soap formulations to minimize contact allergy risk.

rules.name: Sodium Chloride (Salt) Limit
rules.description: Sodium Chloride (NaCl) must not exceed 0.5% in Grade 1 toilet soap — excess salt degrades lather quality and soap structure (BIS IS 2888:2004).

rules.name: Glycerin (Humectant) Limit
rules.description: Glycerin in toilet soap should not exceed 5% — excess glycerin causes softening, sweating, and reduced shelf life.

rules.name: EDTA (Chelating Agent) Limit
rules.description: EDTA chelating agent must not exceed 0.1% due to environmental persistence and aquatic toxicity concerns.

rules.name: Heavy Metals Cumulative Limit
rules.description: Cumulative sum of Lead + Arsenic must not exceed 22 ppm — combined heavy metal burden limit.

rules.name: Total Fragrance Limit
rules.description: Total fragrance compounds in rinse-off cosmetics must not exceed 1% — EU Cosmetics Regulation 1223/2009 and IFRA guidelines.

rules.name: Total Colorant Limit
rules.description: Combined colorant content (dyes, pigments, oxides) must not exceed 0.5% for a visually acceptable and regulatory-safe product.
